rows.definition: internal/recall's Manifest: the persisted core image of a finished session - the page table, the frozen quarantine-clearance state, and a frozen world-version marker, with the bytes themselves living in a sibling cas.json swap device.
rows.distinction: The session SNAPSHOT schema (recall.Manifest) - NOT the policy manifest (internal/policy's on-disk capability-floor JSON); same token 'Manifest', two unrelated meanings in two unrelated packages.

rows.definition: internal/policy's Manifest: the on-disk capability-floor JSON an operator edits to configure the authorization intent - the abstract policy is compiled from this into an in-memory decision table.
rows.distinction: The on-disk authorization CONFIG (policy.Manifest) - NOT the session core-image manifest (recall.Manifest); same token 'Manifest', a capability-floor schema vs a session snapshot.

rows.definition: internal/session's Verdict: what Decide returns to the agent turn loop at each boundary - Proceed (gate the next turn), MaxTokens (per-turn output cap), the drive State, Stop, and a closed Reason naming why the slot freed.
rows.distinction: The per-turn LOOP gate (session.Verdict) - NOT abi.Verdict (internal/abi's discriminated-union adjudication decision); same token 'Verdict', a turn-boundary control value vs a tool-call authorization decision.

rows.definition: internal/abi's Verdict: the discriminated-union adjudication decision type (Allow, Deny, Defer, Transform, Quarantine, RequireWitness) - the kernel's tool-call authorization decision surface.
rows.distinction: The adjudication DECISION type (abi.Verdict) - NOT session.Verdict (the per-turn loop gate); same token 'Verdict', a tool-call authorization decision vs a turn-boundary control value.

rows.definition: internal/compute's Backend: the small whole-op HAL interface the forward loop targets (MatMul, RMSNorm, RoPE, Attention, NewKV ...), carrying a Tier capability probe and a CorrectnessClass; the CPU reference delegates to the model's exact arithmetic.
rows.distinction: The hardware-abstraction (HAL) interface for model math - NOT memq's Backend (the memory-cell + trust-gated byte-access supplier); same token 'Backend', a compute-primitive surface vs a memory-cell source.

rows.definition: internal/memq's Backend: supplies memory cells (the page table as SAFE-metadata cells) and trust-gated byte access (Materialize pages one cell's bytes in through the trust gate); optional Tombstoner/Pruner add the durable mutations a backend chooses to support.
rows.distinction: The memory-cell + trust-gated byte source - NOT compute's Backend (the model-math HAL interface); same token 'Backend', a cell/page-in supplier vs a tensor-op primitive surface.

rows.definition: internal/rungobs's Observer: the passive rung-decision distribution counter - registered via abi.RegisterEmitter, it dedups by call SeqNo and histograms adjudication decisions (rung x kind x reason), read out with Snapshot.
rows.distinction: The adjudication decision-DISTRIBUTION histogram - NOT cacheobs's Observer (the KV-prefix REUSE accumulator); same token 'Observer', counting the verdict mix vs accumulating prefill reuse.

rows.definition: internal/cacheobs's Observer: accumulates in-kernel KV-prefix reuse across served turns - prompt vs reused tokens, bucketed frozen/partial/cold by reuse ratio; the gateway scrapes the process-global Default.
rows.distinction: The KV-prefix REUSE accumulator - NOT rungobs's Observer (the adjudication decision-distribution counter); same token 'Observer', tracking prefill reuse vs counting the verdict mix.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's Index: the planner's candidate access path over a history store - an inverted token index, the append/recency order, and the durable set, maintained incrementally so a Probe returns a BOUNDED candidate set without rescanning all spans; metadata-only and deterministic.
rows.distinction: The metadata-only candidate ACCESS PATH for context planning - NOT simhash's Index (the brute-force nearest-neighbor vector store); same token 'Index', an inverted span index vs a cosine-similarity corpus.

rows.definition: internal/simhash's Index: an in-memory brute-force nearest-neighbor store - add (id, Vector, meta), then TopK(query, k) for the k most similar by cosine; a deterministic linear scan sized for a trajectory corpus of thousands of rows.
rows.distinction: The cosine nearest-neighbor VECTOR store - NOT ctxplan's Index (the planner's inverted candidate access path); same token 'Index', a similarity corpus vs a span-selection index.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's Store: the history image the planner views - it supplies spans (SAFE metadata) and trust-gated byte access (Materialize is the gated page-in; a sealed span's bytes never cross the gate). A real deployment backs it with a recall core image or a memq backend.
rows.distinction: The planner's SPAN-supplying, trust-gated history interface - NOT blob's Store (the content-addressed blob CAS); same token 'Store', a gated span view vs a digest->bytes content store.

rows.definition: internal/blob's Store: the content-addressed blob store (CAS) - digest->bytes with pin-aware bounded eviction (pinned digests, e.g. a vDSO tier-2 entry or a held quarantine handle, are never evicted), preserving the 'a cache hit equals a fresh call' invariant.
rows.distinction: The digest->bytes content-addressed CAS - NOT ctxplan's Store (the planner's gated span/history interface); same token 'Store', a blob CAS vs a span view.

rows.definition: internal/ctxplan's Refusal: a selected span the trust gate declined to page in (sealed, or its bytes went missing) - reported to the caller, never rendered into the fresh history.
rows.distinction: A planner SPAN the gate declined to page in - NOT memq's Refusal (a memory CELL an effect declined to touch); same token 'Refusal', a context-span page-in refusal vs an effect-cell refusal.

rows.definition: internal/memq's Refusal: a cell an effect declined to touch (sealed / tombstoned / page-in refused) - recorded with a reason on the executor's result.
rows.distinction: A memory CELL an effect declined to touch - NOT ctxplan's Refusal (a planner span the gate declined to page in); same token 'Refusal', an effect-cell refusal vs a context-span page-in refusal.
